body: This is a new requirement for the following liquor licences: Liquor licensees must ensure any individual delivering liquor on their behalf isServing It Right (SIR)certified, including employees or third-party delivery drivers. When a licensee wants to use a third-party delivery service to deliver liquor, the licensee should confirm: Once the licensee confirms these measures are in place, they donothave to check every driver’s SIR certification when orders are picked up for delivery. Licensees should ensure the SIR requirement for delivery drivers is documented in their agreements with third-party delivery service providers. However, licensees are not required to keep SIR records for third-party delivery drivers. Common carriers (e.g., Canada Post, FedEx) are exempt from the requirement to be SIR certified. Additionally, delivery services arenotrequired to deliver liquor on the same day it is purchased. Previously, only Food Primary and Liquor Primary licensees selling liquor with a meal for delivery were required to confirm SIR certification for delivery providers. This public safety requirement creates consistency across all licence classes offering liquor delivery. The updates can be found in the: Most delivery service providers already require their drivers to be SIR certified. Generally, their online platform stores the driver’s SIR certification and only pushes orders that include liquor to drivers with valid SIR certification (meaning SIR that has been verified by the company and is not expired). However, it is the licensee’s ultimate responsibility to confirm the third-party delivery service has these measures in place for their drivers because they hold the liquor licence. For record keeping, licensees arenotrequired to keep SIR records for third-party delivery drivers because they are not employees of the licensee.

minutes_briefs.title: All liquor delivery drivers must now be Serving It Right certified
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia now requires that anyone delivering liquor on behalf of a licensed establishment — including employees and third-party delivery drivers — must hold a valid Serving It Right (SIR) certification. This rule applies to all liquor licence classes, not just Food Primary and Liquor Primary as before. Licensees must confirm that their third-party delivery service has measures in place to ensure only SIR-certified drivers deliver liquor, but they do not need to check each driver's certification individually or keep SIR records for third-party drivers. Common carriers like Canada Post are exempt.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Update your agreements with third-party delivery services to include the SIR certification requirement for all liquor delivery drivers.
